select_when: A problem is attracting a fast blame story and the team needs to resist assigning causation before the structure is actually understood.
The current diagnosis may be confusing symptom, proximate trigger, and root cause because the first explanation feels morally or emotionally satisfying.
An incident or outcome needs structural cause tracing before responsibility, remediation, or redesign decisions are made.
The next move should separate hidden drivers from the most narratively available explanation.
avoid_when: The team is using anti-blame language to avoid naming an obvious causal driver, accountable party, or concrete design flaw.
The situation already has good causal evidence and now needs action rather than another loop of attribution skepticism.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model focused on resisting fast blame stories, separating proximate from root causes, and forcing causal discipline before responsibility hardens.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor causality sprawl because the raw source is stronger on anti-blame narrative resistance, root-driver tracing, and structural diagnosis.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that also spans accountability design, investigative method, and systems diagnosis.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on resisting neat attribution stories, distinguishing proximate from root causes, and tracing structure before judgment.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are grounded in source warnings that attribution skepticism can also become avoidance once causal evidence is already strong.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on blame-first causal error, while the typed input is a compact reviewed label.

output_type.note: The source yields disciplined causal diagnosis rather than a single culprit story; the output label compresses that result.

reasoning_types.note: The model is strongest as causal and diagnostic reasoning, while the taxonomy names remain normalized.
